purpose: Applies Minsky's financial instability framework to covenant-lite credit markets to predict default cascade dynamics and timeline compression.
components: Hedge finance: borrower can meet all obligations from cash flows
Speculative finance: borrower can meet interest but not principal (needs refinancing) — traditionally a warning phase
Ponzi finance: borrower cannot meet interest (needs asset appreciation or forbearance)
Covenant-lite impact: eliminates speculative phase by removing covenant triggers that historically forced early restructuring
Direct hedge-to-Ponzi transition prediction: cliff-edge rather than gradual default emergence
current_assessment: Theoretically sound — covenant-lite removes the early warning triggers that historically delineated speculative phase. Whether this produces cliff-edge or gradual defaults in organic downturn is literally unknown. Specific 3-6 month timeline was refuted as empirically uncalibrated. Called strongest theoretical contribution by pair_1.
limitations: Zero organic downturn observations for covenant-lite regime
COVID test confounded by $800B+ PPP and Fed purchases
Specific timeline compression (3-6 months) was refuted — honest statement is likely compressed, magnitude unknown
Bimodal vs smooth default distribution genuinely untestable ex ante
